headlines.title: SpaceX targets fixed $135 IPO roadshow price at $1.75 trillion valuation, source says
headlines.summary: The valuation would make Elon Musk's company the seventh- biggest in the U.S. and above Tesla, which has a market cap of about $1.6 trillion.

headlines.title: Sellers are pulling homes off the market at the fastest pace since 2020
headlines.summary: Frustrated sellers are pulling their homes off the market at an increasingly high pace, as demand weakens and bidding wars wane.

headlines.title: Private payrolls grew by 122,000 in May, stronger than expected, ADP reports
headlines.summary: Unlike prior months, where job growth was concentrated in healthcare and a few other sectors, gains were more broad-based.

headlines.title: GOP Sen. Tillis slams Trump intelligence pick Pulte: 'Don't think he has a prayer'
headlines.summary: Pulte is widely viewed as a Trump loyalist who has targeted the president's political foes during his tenure leading the housing regulatory agency.

headlines.title: Morgan Stanley will soon open its trillion-dollar wealth management funnel to AI agents
headlines.summary: Morgan Stanley's move is one of the earliest instances of a major Wall Street bank opening its platforms to external AI tools.

headlines.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to meet with lawmakers, Trump officials in D.C.
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump signed an executive order about AI this week, which Altman voiced support for.

headlines.title: Leveraged ETF assets double in two months as investors press AI bet
headlines.summary: Investors turn to ETFs linked to AI and Tech, with exposures in the U.S. and Korea/Taiwan doubling in just two months

headlines.title: Bitcoin trails stocks by most since 2019 as traders get their kicks elsewhere
headlines.summary: Bitcoin hasn't had this cold a winter in seven years.

headlines.title: Software stocks just passed a big milestone
headlines.summary: Software stocks are making a comeback from their artificial intelligence-driven sell-off earlier this year.

headlines.title: Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alphabet, deepening bet on AI
headlines.summary: The deal builds on a stake Berkshire has steadily expanded since late 2025, making Alphabet one of its biggest recent bets.

headlines.title: Greg Abel just made his first big deal as Berkshire CEO. Why Warren Buffett is happy
headlines.summary: Berkshire's purchase of homebuilder Taylor Morrison gives the conglomerate a larger foothold in housing and appears to have been struck at a bargain valuation.

headlines.title: Ralph Lauren shares have flatlined in 2026. Here's how to profit anyway
headlines.summary: When investors sell strangles, they are selling an out of the money put and an out of the money call.

headlines.title: Looking to buy into the SpaceX IPO? This scary chart might make you think twice.
headlines.summary: Stock performance following previous major tech IPOs has been less than stellar.

headlines.title: You’re over 40 and laid off. Is it age discrimination? What you can do.
headlines.summary: Age discrimination is notoriously hard to prove. Here is what to watch out for.

headlines.title: Marvell’s stock is on a run not seen in a quarter-century as the tech company grows in stature
headlines.summary: Marvell now ranks No. 22 in the S&P 500 with a $269 billion market capitalization that’s higher than those of PepsiCo and T-Mobile.

headlines.title: I am 71. Would it be foolish to sell $10,000 in stock to visit my grandchildren in Thailand?
headlines.summary: “I am comfortable living on my Social Security and pension income.”

headlines.title: The Iran war hasn’t slowed the economy, but higher inflation leads companies to freeze hiring
headlines.summary: The largest part of the economy grew faster in May, even as businesses were coping with the worst inflation in several years — at a cost to job seekers. Many companies have adopted temporary hiring freezes to offset their own rising costs.

headlines.title: Bill Ackman sees investors repeating a mistake of 2000: Flocking to the ‘new new’ and ignoring quality names
headlines.summary: Billionaire hedge-fund manager Bill Ackman says investors are ignoring Microsoft and other Big Tech names for the sake of the “new new thing,” like chip stocks.
